source: Buy-side diligence question list (demo). Modeled assumptions reflect a fund's pre-call note.
thesis: CMG is a high-return unit-growth compounder: Chipotlanes and throughput push new-unit economics toward a ~7,000-restaurant North American runway, restaurant-level margins hold in the mid-to-high 20s through food and labor inflation, and digital plus loyalty deepen the moat.

questions.pillar: Unit-growth runway: the ~7,000 NA restaurant target is real and new-unit returns are holding
questions.question: You've guided to ~7,000 North American restaurants long term — what's the current new-unit pace, and are Chipotlanes still the majority of openings with better returns?
complete_when: States the current new-unit pace or growth rate
Confirms the long-term North American unit target
Addresses Chipotlane mix and whether new-unit returns are holding

expected.note_models: 8-10% net new units per year toward a ~7,000 NA target
expected.our_view: Runway intact as long as new-unit cash-on-cash returns hold
expected.watch: New-unit cash-on-cash returns and Chipotlane share of openings

questions.pillar: Transaction-led comps: comp growth is traffic-driven, not mostly price
questions.question: How much of recent comp growth is transactions versus the menu pricing you've carried over the past year?
complete_when: Splits the comp into transactions versus price

expected.note_models: ~3-4 points of comp from menu pricing taken over the trailing year
expected.our_view: Traffic-led, but carried pricing is a real chunk of the comp
expected.bear: If the comp is mostly price, it is far less durable than it looks

questions.pillar: Throughput: initiatives are lifting peak-hour transactions
questions.question: What throughput initiatives are live, and are they actually lifting peak-hour transactions?
complete_when: Gives evidence of peak-hour transaction gains

expected.our_view: Throughput is the underrated comp lever

questions.pillar: Margin durability: restaurant-level margins hold in the mid-to-high 20s through inflation
questions.question: Where are restaurant-level margins running, and can they hold against beef and wage inflation?
complete_when: Addresses how beef and labor inflation are offset

expected.our_view: Margins durable if pricing plus throughput offset input inflation
expected.risk: Beef inflation outruns pricing and compresses restaurant margin

questions.pillar: Digital and loyalty: the digital mix is durable and loyalty is being monetized
questions.question: How durable is the digital mix, and how are you monetizing the loyalty base?
complete_when: States the digital mix of sales

expected.thesis: Digital and loyalty deepen the moat and lift frequency

questions.pillar: Bounded downside: value perception protects traffic in a consumer slowdown
questions.question: What's the single biggest risk — if the consumer pulls back, does value perception hold your traffic?
complete_when: Addresses value perception and traffic resilience in a slowdown

expected.bear_case: A consumer pullback hits traffic and the premium price point
expected.base_fact: Value perception has historically kept traffic resilient versus peers
